title: Check the following for grammar and duplication: Regarding you stating that I should contact roadside assistance if I be…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Check the following for grammar and duplication: Regarding you stating that I should contact roadside assistance if I believe the vehicle is unsafe to drive demonstrates a blatant avoidance of your legal responsibilities and liabilities. I have already informed you of the safety issues arising when I use the vehicle’s inbuilt features for the intended purpose, rendering the car unsafe to drive. By suggesting I refrain from using these features to maintain a safe driving experience, you are effectively denying me the product I initially purchased and contradicting the vehicle’s intended use and purpose.
Despite the facts remaining unchanged, you are persistently ignoring and deflecting your obligations, legal responsibilities, and liabilities back to me. I have previously informed you of the safety problems, which render the car unsafe to drive when utilising the inbuilt features for their intended purpose. As I mentioned in my first email on 6/5/2023, these issues create life-threatening situations. The vehicle is not fit for purpose, and you continue to deny me my legal rights as an Australian consumer.
The vehicle I purchased from you at LDV was intended to cater to my needs by offering these specific features, which was one of the main reasons for buying it. However, the dysfunctional and faulty features make the vehicle unsafe and dangerous to drive when used as intended. Your incongruous reasoning is akin to me having bought a vehicle without those features, only to add them on later for necessity and find that they compromise the vehicle’s safety. This situation is not what I anticipated when purchasing the vehicle from you, and it compounds my frustration with your unwillingness to resolve the issue effectively.

This is the only item that we agree on, except that he is refusing to honour that commitment. 
Regarding the following he stated, “the assessment if it turns out to be a non-warrantable item, as I have seen in the past, items can be affected from outside influences including but not limited to aftermarket wiring, dash camera, usb with incorrect data, damage from pests, these circumstances are when it would be paid by yourself.” All those issues and problems I have been dealing with most of them after from after I took delivery of the vehicle, they became most of the issues raised, evident at that time. They were raised with LDV Lansvale dealer on the 16/09/2021 at the time of the first complimentary service of which they said the software was updated, it had fixed the problems. But was the was not true. And ever since I keep getting the same story computer upgrade will solve the problems. So, I completely refute your claim that it is my fault or some other related issue that I am responsible to pay for, otherwise they would have been fixed when LDV Parramatta, last serviced my car, which included a list of those warranty faults. Only LDV service personnel have worked on the car. }

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Regarding the statement he made, "As for your rights, you are correct that an accepted warranty claim for a manufacturer defect is to be repaired at no cost to yourself," we do agree on this point. However, he is refusing to honour that commitment. 

He also stated that "the assessment, if it turns out to be a non-warrantable item, as I have seen in the past, items can be affected by outside influences, including but not limited to aftermarket wiring, dash cameras, USBs with incorrect data, damage from pests; these circumstances are when it would be paid by yourself." I have been dealing with most of these issues and problems since I took delivery of the vehicle, and they became evident at that time. I raised these issues with the LDV Lansvale dealer on 16/09/2021 during the first complimentary service, and they informed me that a software update had fixed the problems. However, this was not true. Since then, I keep getting the same story that a computer upgrade will solve the problems. 

Therefore, I completely refute your claim that these issues are my fault or that they are related to some other factor for which I should be held responsible to pay. If this were the case, then the issues would have been resolved when LDV Parramatta last serviced my car, which included a list of those warranty faults. Yet again, I received the same response that a computer upgrade had resolved the problems, but it did not. I can also confirm that there has been no aftermarket wiring, and only LDV service personnel have worked on the car.

Your acknowledgement that I am correct and have the right to have manufacturer defects repaired at no cost is the only point of agreement. However, you are refusing to honour that commitment. Regarding your statement, "if the assessment turns out to be a non-warrantable item, as I have seen in the past, items can be affected by outside influences including but not limited to aftermarket wiring, dash cameras, USBs with incorrect data, damage from pests; these circumstances are when it would be paid by yourself," I want to clarify that these issues and problems were present from when I took delivery of the vehicle and have been evident since then.

I raised these concerns with the LDV Lansvale dealer on 16/09/2021 during the first complimentary service, and they informed me that the car's software was updated to fix the problems. However, that was not the case, because ever since, I keep getting told the same story that a computer upgrade will resolve the issues. So, I completely refute your claim that it is my fault or some other related problem that I am responsible for paying for; otherwise, they would have been fixed when LDV Parramatta last serviced my car, which included a list of those warranty faults. Again, I received a similar answer that a computer upgrade had resolved the problems, but this is not true as the issues still persist and are, in fact, worse.

I can also confirm that there has been no aftermarket wiring, as only LDV service personnel have worked on the car.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Check the following for grammar and flow using English AUD: {Regarding the following you said by email, “At no stage are you being denied an assessment of the vehicle, and if at any point you are unsatisfied with the assessment or wish to try for an earlier booking, your warranty is valid Australia wide at any Authorised LDV Australia service centre”.  That statement is false, as you have constantly denied repairing the vehicles warranty problems, pressuring me wanting me to sign a document that I have to pay $165 per item, to assess the faults of the vehicle, for you then to determine if the faults are covered by warranty, otherwise you will not assess the warranty issues, off which all these problems that I have been experiencing are from the time the vehicle was purchased. Further, as you have already verbally stated to me, my issues are not covered by warranty, because that is just the way the car is, that’s the way you bought it. In other words, you have sold me a car which is not fit for purpose which is contrary to all your promotion and the features the car contains, not working as they should and appropriate acceptable standard. In complete breach of my consumer rights. You and LDV are trying to intimidate me acting like a bully, expecting me to pay for the assessment and then pay to repair what are regarded as warrant issues}

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Regarding your email statement, "At no stage are you being denied an assessment of the vehicle, and if at any point you are unsatisfied with the assessment or wish to try for an earlier booking, your warranty is valid Australia-wide at any Authorised LDV Australia service centre," I must point out that this statement is false. You have constantly denied repairing the vehicle's warranty problems and pressured me into signing a document stating that I have to pay $165 per item for assessing the faults of the vehicle. Only after that would you determine if the faults are covered by warranty; otherwise, you will not assess the warranty issues. All these problems I have been experiencing started from the time the vehicle was purchased.

Furthermore, you have already verbally stated to me that my issues are not covered by warranty, simply because "that's just the way the car is; that's how you bought it." In other words, you have sold me a car that is not fit for purpose, which contradicts your promotions and the features the car contains. The features are not working to an appropriate and acceptable standard, putting you in complete breach of my consumer rights. You and LDV are trying to intimidate me, acting like bullies, expecting me to pay for the assessment and then pay to repair what are regarded as warranty issues.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: can it be improved?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
In response to your email statement, "At no stage are you being denied an assessment of the vehicle, and if at any point you are unsatisfied with the assessment or wish to try for an earlier booking, your warranty is valid Australia-wide at any Authorised LDV Australia service centre," I must highlight that this statement is inaccurate. You have consistently refused to address the vehicle's warranty issues and pressured me into signing a document that states I must pay $165 per item for fault assessment. Only after this will you determine if the faults are covered under warranty. If not, you will not assess the warranty issues, even though all these problems have persisted since the vehicle's purchase.

Moreover, you have verbally informed me that my concerns are not covered by warranty because "that's just the way the car is; that's how you bought it." Essentially, you have sold me a car that is not fit for its intended purpose. This discrepancy contradicts your promotional claims and the non-functioning features that are expected to meet appropriate and acceptable standards, constituting a complete violation of my consumer rights. Both you and LDV are attempting to intimidate me by acting like bullies, expecting me to pay for both the assessment and the subsequent repair of issues that should be considered covered under warranty.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
In response to your email statement, "At no stage are you being denied an assessment of the vehicle, and if at any point you are unsatisfied with the assessment or wish to try for an earlier booking, your warranty is valid Australia-wide at any Authorised LDV Australia service centre," I must emphasise that this statement is misleading. Despite acknowledging that my warranty is valid, you have consistently refused to address the vehicle's warranty issues. Instead, you pressured me into signing a document that obliges me to pay $165 per item for fault assessment, after which you will determine if the faults are covered under the warranty. This approach is unreasonable, considering that all these problems have persisted since the vehicle's purchase.

Your assertion that my concerns are not covered by the warranty due to "that's just the way the car is; that's how you bought it" is unacceptable. It implies that you have sold me a car that is not fit for its intended purpose. This discrepancy contradicts your promotional claims and raises concerns about the non-functioning features expected to meet appropriate and acceptable standards, amounting to a severe breach of my consumer rights. Both you and LDV are attempting to intimidate me by acting like bullies, expecting me to pay for both the assessment and the subsequent repair of issues that should be considered covered under the warranty.

Furthermore, I find it perplexing that you suggest I take the car to another LDV service centre to address warranty problems, while simultaneously dismissing any responsibility for the issues. It is important to mention that your predecessor, Jamie Procopio, was helpful and understanding regarding these warranty issues, demonstrating good faith and customer care that has now unfortunately disappeared.

My experience with Jamie and the LDV Manager at Lansvale revealed that many other LDV T60 vehicles face the same issues, implying a manufacturing problem that makes the car unfit for purpose. By brushing off these genuine concerns, you exacerbate my anxieties and stresses, displaying a lack of customer care, support, and compassion in dealing with this issue. It is vital that you acknowledge the manufacturing problem and honour the warranty to maintain the goodwill and trust that LDV has cultivated.

turns.text:
Can the following be improved, supporting my argument. check grammar in english aud:   {Re: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Unacceptable Customer Service - LDV T60- Vehicle Reg. ENL76J
Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,
I am extremely disappointed by your persistent disregard for my concerns, specific requests, and consumer rights. It is evident you are ignoring what I have said, refusing to reply to my specific concerns and requests I have raised. Your actions are denying me my consumer rights and obstructing the proper warranty requirement method for dealing and resolving these issues. You and LDV Parramatta are neglecting my legal rights as an Australian consumer and have failed to adhere to warranty protocols and practices which is unacceptable behaviour.
Regarding your repeated explanation as to your insistent decision on charging an inspection and diagnostic fee, we have already communicated this during our phone call on 10/5/23, and I have informed you in my last email that I am in total disagreement with your assessment of this fee and how I expect the issue to be handled. Despite this, you have persistently avoided responding to my specific concerns and neglected to follow the warranty requirement legislation, denying me my consumer rights. 
You have further aggravated the matter by your consistent evasion of my request to provide me with your general manager and directors’ contact information. This only adds to my frustration with your lack of transparency and unwillingness to resolve these matters efficiently. This is absolutely unacceptable.
You statement stating that I contact roadside assistance if I believe the vehicle is unsafe to drive demonstrates a blatant avoidance of your legal responsibilities and liabilities. I have already informed you of the vehicle’s built-in features that render the car unsafe to drive. By suggesting I refrain from using these features to maintain a safe driving experience, you are effectively denying me the product I initially purchased and contradicting the vehicle’s intended use and purpose.
I purchased this vehicle from LDV as it catered to my needs by offering these specific features, which was one of the main reasons for buying it. However, the dysfunctional and faulty features make the vehicle unsafe and dangerous to drive when used as intended. Your incongruous rationale is akin to me having bought a vehicle without those features, only to add them later for necessity and find that they compromise the vehicle’s safety. This situation is not what I anticipated when purchasing the vehicle from you, and it compounds my frustration with your unwillingness to resolve these issues.
As I first mentioned on 6/5/2023, these issues have created life-threatening situations. The vehicle is not fit for its purpose, and you continue to deny me my legal rights as an Australian consumer.

Your acknowledgement that I am correct and have the right to have the manufacturer defects repaired at no cost is the only point of agreement. However, you are refusing to honour that commitment.
Regarding your explanation that “if the assessment turns out to be a non-warrantable item etc… it would be paid by yourself.” I want to clarify that these problems started from when I took delivery of the vehicle and have since then been evident. I raised these concerns with the LDV Lansvale dealer on 16/09/2021 during the first service and they informed me that the car’s software was updated to fix the problems. However, that did not work.
Since then, I keep getting the same story, the issues are computer related and an upgrade will resolve the problems. Therefore, I completely refute your claim that it is different related issue, that I may be responsible to pay for. If that was the case, these issues would have been resolved when LDV Parramatta last serviced my car, as it included most of those warranty faults. But again, I was told that the computer upgrade had resolved the problems. Unfortunately, the problems are persisting and getting worse. I also confirm, there has been no after-market wiring as only LDV service personnel have worked on the car.
In response to your assertion, “At no stage are you being denied an assessment of the vehicle, and if at any point you are unsatisfied with the assessment or wish to try for an earlier booking, your warranty is valid Australia-wide at any Authorised LDV Australia service centre,” I must emphasise that this statement is misleading. Despite acknowledging that my warranty is valid, you have consistently refused to address the vehicle’s warranty issues. Instead, you are pressuring me into signing a document that obliges me to pay $165 per item for fault assessment, after which you will determine if the faults are covered under the warranty. This approach is unreasonable, considering that all these problems have persisted since the vehicle’s purchase.
Your assertion that my concerns are not covered by the warranty due to “that’s just the way the car is; that’s how you bought it” is unacceptable. It implies that you have sold me a car that is not fit for its intended purpose. This discrepancy contradicts your promotional claims and raises concerns about the non-functioning features expected to meet appropriate and acceptable standards, amounting to a severe breach of my consumer rights. Both you and LDV are attempting to intimidate me by acting like bullies, expecting me to pay for both the assessment and the subsequent repair of issues that should be covered under the warranty.
Furthermore, I find it perplexing that you suggest I take the car to another LDV service centre to address warranty problems, while simultaneously dismissing any responsibility for the issues. It is important to mention that your predecessor, Jamie Procopio, was helpful and understanding regarding these warranty issues, demonstrating good faith and customer care that has now unfortunately disappeared.
My experience with Jamie and the LDV Manager at Lansvale revealed that other LDV T60 vehicles face the same issues, implying a manufacturing problem that makes the car unfit for purpose. By brushing off these genuine concerns, you exacerbate my anxieties and stresses, displaying a lack of customer care, support, and compassion in dealing with this issue. It is vital that you acknowledge the manufacturing problem and honour the warranty to maintain the goodwill and trust that LDV has cultivated. It is also essential that you provide me with your general manager and directors’ contact information, which I have now requested for a third time.
I am appalled by the lack of care and support you have demonstrated, worsening my anxieties and stress over your uncompassionate handling of this matter, which is clearly related to manufacturing issues and a vehicle unfit for its intended purpose.
In addition, yesterday the display screen display turned purple on ignition, not able to access any functions. I had to drive with a purple screen without access to vehicles function. It eventually, reset itself after an hour. This is a continuations of more similar malfunctioning problems with vehicle causing me intense grief whenever I use the vehicle.     
I strongly urge you to deal with my concerns in line with consumer rights and warranty protocols, rather than deflecting responsibility and dodging my specific requests. Now more than ever, I expect a responsible, understanding, and transparent approach towards resolving this matter that has been causing me significant distress. 
Regards,
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>}

Re: Escalating Issue – Vehicle Repairs and Unacceptable Customer Service - LDV T60 - Vehicle Reg. ENL76J
Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I am extremely disappointed by your persistent disregard for my concerns, specific requests, and consumer rights. It is evident that you are ignoring what I have said and refusing to reply to my specific concerns and requests I have raised. Your actions deny me my consumer rights and obstruct the proper warranty requirement method for dealing with and resolving these issues. You and LDV Parramatta are neglecting my legal rights as an Australian consumer and have failed to adhere to warranty protocols and practices which is unacceptable behaviour.

Regarding your repeated explanation as to your insistent decision on charging an inspection and diagnostic fee, we have already communicated this during our phone call on 10/5/23, and I have informed you in my last email that I am in total disagreement with your assessment of this fee and how I expect the issue to be handled. Despite this, you have persistently avoided responding to my specific concerns and neglected to follow the warranty requirement legislation, denying me my consumer rights.

You have further aggravated the matter by your consistent evasion of my request to provide me with your general manager and directors’ contact information. This only adds to my frustration with your lack of transparency and unwillingness to resolve these matters efficiently. This is absolutely unacceptable.

Your statement stating that I contact roadside assistance if I believe the vehicle is unsafe to drive demonstrates a blatant avoidance of your legal responsibilities and liabilities. I have already informed you of the vehicle’s built-in features that render the car unsafe to drive. By suggesting I refrain from using these features to maintain a safe driving experience, you are effectively denying me the product I initially purchased and contradicting the vehicle’s intended use and purpose.

I purchased this vehicle from LDV as it catered to my needs by offering these specific features, which was one of the main reasons for buying it. However, the dysfunctional and faulty features make the vehicle unsafe and dangerous to drive when used as intended. Your incongruous rationale is akin to me having bought a vehicle without those features, only to add them later for necessity and find that they compromise the vehicle’s safety. This situation is not what I anticipated when purchasing the vehicle from you, and it compounds my frustration with your unwillingness to resolve these issues.

As I first mentioned on 6/5/2023, these issues have created life-threatening situations. The vehicle is not fit for its purpose, and you continue to deny me my legal rights as an Australian consumer.

Your acknowledgement that I am correct and have the right to have the manufacturer defects repaired at no cost is the only point of agreement. However, you are refusing to honour that commitment.

Regarding your explanation that “if the assessment turns out to be a non-warrantable item etc… it would be paid by yourself.” I want to clarify that these problems started from when I took delivery of the vehicle and have since then been evident. I raised these concerns with the LDV Lansvale dealer on 16/09/2021 during the first service, and they informed me that the car’s software was updated to fix the problems. However, that did not work.

Since then, I keep getting the same story that the issues are computer-related, and an upgrade will resolve the problems. Therefore, I completely refute your claim that it is a different related issue that I may be responsible to pay for. If that was the case, these issues would have been resolved when LDV Parramatta last serviced my car, as it included most of those warranty faults. But again, I was told that the computer upgrade had resolved the problems. Unfortunately, the problems are persisting and getting worse. I also confirm there has been no after-market wiring as only LDV service personnel have worked on the car.

In response to your assertion, “At no stage are you being denied an assessment of the vehicle, and if at any point you are unsatisfied with the assessment or wish to try for an earlier booking, your warranty is valid Australia-wide at any Authorised LDV Australia service centre,” I must emphasise that this statement is misleading. Despite acknowledging that my warranty is valid, you have consistently refused to address the vehicle’s warranty issues. Instead, you are pressuring me into signing a document that obliges me to pay $165 per item for fault assessment, after which you will determine if the faults are covered under the warranty. This approach is unreasonable, considering that all these problems have persisted since the vehicle’s purchase.

Your assertion that my concerns are not covered by the warranty due to “that’s just the way the car is; that’s how you bought it” is unacceptable. It implies that you have sold me a car that is not fit for its intended purpose. This discrepancy contradicts your promotional claims and raises concerns about the non-functioning features expected to meet appropriate and acceptable standards, amounting to a severe breach of my consumer rights. Both you and LDV are attempting to intimidate me by acting like bullies, expecting me to pay for both the assessment and the subsequent repair of issues that should be covered under the warranty.

Furthermore, I find it perplexing that you suggest I take the car to another LDV service centre to address warranty problems, while simultaneously dismissing any responsibility for the issues. By brushing off these genuine concerns, you exacerbate my anxieties and stresses, displaying a lack of customer care, support, and compassion in dealing with this issue. It is vital that you acknowledge the manufacturing problem and honour the warranty to maintain the goodwill and trust that LDV has cultivated. It is also essential that you provide me with your general manager and directors’ contact information, which I have now requested for a third time.
